exams.for_colleges: NITs, IIITs, GFTIs (Government Funded Technical Institutes), and many state/private engineering colleges across India
exams.eligibility: 12th pass or appearing with PCM. No age limit. Maximum 3 consecutive years of attempts from year of passing 12th.
subjects: Each section: 20 MCQs + 5 Numerical Value questions
+4 for correct, -1 for wrong MCQ, no negative for numerical
exams.preparation_tips: Start in Class 11. Master NCERT textbooks first, then move to HC Verma (Physics), MS Chouhan (Chemistry), RD Sharma & Cengage (Maths). Practice previous year papers. Take mock tests regularly. Focus on time management — 3 hours for 75 questions. January attempt for practice, April for best score.

exams.conducting_body: One of the 7 IITs (rotational basis) under JAB (Joint Admission Board)
exams.for_colleges: 23 IITs across India (IIT Bombay, Delhi, Madras, Kanpur, Kharagpur, Roorkee, etc.)
exams.eligibility: Must be in top 2,50,000 qualifiers of JEE Main. Age limit: born on or after October 1, 2001 (General). Maximum 2 attempts in consecutive years. Must have scored 75% in 12th (65% for SC/ST).
subjects: Mix of MCQ (single correct, multiple correct), Numerical, and Matrix Match questions
exams.preparation_tips: JEE Advanced requires deeper conceptual understanding than JEE Main. Focus on Irodov & DC Pandey (Physics), JD Lee & Pankaj Singh (Chemistry), Arihant & Vikas Gupta (Maths). Solve previous 20 years' papers. Join a good coaching or follow structured online courses. IIT Roorkee is the dream college for Uttarakhand students — ranks around 1500-5000 can get admission.

exams.for_colleges: All government & private medical/dental colleges in India including AIIMS and JIPMER (merged into NEET)
exams.eligibility: 12th pass or appearing with PCB (Physics, Chemistry, Biology). Minimum 50% in PCB aggregate (40% for SC/ST/OBC). Minimum age 17 years. No upper age limit (as per Supreme Court ruling).
subjects: Total: 180 questions from 200 (choose best 45 from each section of 50)
uttarakhand_colleges_using: Veer Chandra Singh Garhwali Government Medical College Srinagar
exams.preparation_tips: NCERT is the Bible for NEET — read every line including examples and diagrams. Biology carries 360/720 marks, so master it thoroughly. Use MTG, Trueman's for Biology; DC Pandey for Physics; VK Jaiswal/MS Chouhan for Chemistry. Practice 10+ years of previous papers. For Uttarakhand state quota (85%), a score of 450-500 can get government medical college admission.

exams.for_colleges: All government and private medical colleges offering MD/MS across India
exams.eligibility: MBBS degree from a recognized university with completion of internship. Must have permanent/provisional registration with State Medical Council or MCI.
subjects: Para-clinical: Pathology, Pharmacology, Microbiology, Forensic Medicine
Clinical: Medicine, Surgery, OBG, Pediatrics, Orthopedics, ENT, Ophthalmology, etc.
exams.preparation_tips: Start preparation during internship. Use Marrow, PrepLadder, or DAMS for structured study. Focus on high-yield subjects: Medicine, Surgery, OBG, PSM. Revise frequently — NEET PG tests breadth over depth. A score of 450+ opens most government seats. Uttarakhand state quota benefits local MBBS graduates.

exams.conducting_body: Indian Institutes of Management (IIMs) on rotational basis
exams.for_colleges: 20 IIMs, FMS Delhi, MDI Gurgaon, SPJIMR Mumbai, IIT Bombay (SJMSOM), IIT Delhi (DMS), and 1200+ B-Schools accepting CAT scores
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree with minimum 50% marks (45% for SC/ST/PwD) from a recognized university. Final year students can also apply. No age limit.
subjects: VARC - Verbal Ability & Reading Comprehension (24 questions)
DILR - Data Interpretation & Logical Reasoning (20 questions)
Total: 66 questions in 120 minutes (40 mins per section)
+3 for correct MCQ, -1 for wrong MCQ, no negative for TITA (non-MCQ)
exams.preparation_tips: Start 6-8 months before exam. For QA: Arun Sharma, for VARC: read newspapers daily (The Hindu, Economist), for DILR: practice sets from CAT previous years. Take 50+ mock tests (IMS, TIME, Career Launcher). IIM Kashipur is in Uttarakhand — 90+ percentile needed. 99+ percentile for top IIMs. Work experience of 1-3 years improves profile significantly.

exams.conducting_body: IISc Bangalore and 7 IITs jointly (under GATE Committee)
exams.for_colleges: All IITs, NITs, IISc, and other top engineering institutes. Also used by 100+ PSUs (ONGC, IOCL, NTPC, BHEL, PGCIL, etc.) for recruitment.
exams.eligibility: B.Tech/B.E. final year or graduated. Also open to B.Sc, B.Arch, B.Pharm graduates for specific papers. No age limit.
subjects: 30 subject-specific papers available (CS, ME, CE, EE, EC, CH, etc.)
Mix of MCQ (+1/-0.33 or +2/-0.67) and NAT (Numerical Answer Type, no negative)
exams.preparation_tips: Start 6 months before exam. Use Made Easy / ACE Academy study material. For CS: practice programming, algorithms, DBMS, OS, Networks. For core branches: focus on standard textbooks (Signals by Oppenheim, Strength of Materials by Gere, etc.). GATE score valid for 3 years. A top 1000 rank can get IIT Roorkee M.Tech. PSU cutoffs vary — GATE score of 600+ (out of 1000) is competitive for top PSUs.

exams.for_colleges: 22 National Law Universities (NLU Delhi, NLSIU Bangalore, NALSAR Hyderabad, NLU Jodhpur, etc.) and 60+ affiliated law colleges
exams.eligibility: UG (5-year LLB): 12th pass with 45% (40% for SC/ST). No age limit. PG (LLM): LLB/BA LLB with 50% (45% for SC/ST).
subjects: Total: 150 MCQs, +1 for correct, -0.25 for wrong
exams.preparation_tips: Read newspapers daily for Current Affairs & Legal Reasoning (The Hindu, Indian Express). Practice logical reasoning from RS Aggarwal. Study legal GK — landmark judgments, constitutional amendments. For English: read editorials and practice comprehension. CLAT is more about reading speed & comprehension than rote learning. Score 100+ for top NLUs, 70+ for good NLUs.

exams.for_colleges: All 45 Central Universities (DU, JNU, BHU, AMU, etc.), many state universities, and several private universities accepting CUET scores
exams.eligibility: 12th pass or appearing from any recognized board. Specific subject requirements depend on the course and university.
subjects: Section III: General Test (mandatory for some universities)
40-50 MCQs per subject, +5 for correct, -1 for wrong
exams.preparation_tips: CUET is NCERT-based — thoroughly revise Class 12 NCERT for your domain subjects. For General Test: practice GK, logical reasoning, numerical ability, and language skills. Take NTA mock tests available on their website. Choose subjects wisely based on the university/course you want. Score above 200/300 for top central universities.

exams.for_colleges: Central Universities, participating state universities, and select private universities across India
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree in relevant subject from a recognized university. Minimum percentage varies by university and course (typically 50-55%).
exams.preparation_tips: Focus on your graduation-level subject knowledge. Revise key concepts, theories, and formulae. Practice previous year papers of the specific subject you're applying for. For research aptitude section: practice logical reasoning, data interpretation, and research methodology basics.

exams.for_colleges: 600+ B-Schools across India accept MAT scores
exams.frequency: Four times a year (February, May, September, December)
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree from a recognized university. Final year students can also apply. No minimum percentage required.
exams.exam_mode: Online (CBT) and Offline (Paper-Based) — candidate can choose
subjects: Total: 200 questions, +1 for correct, -0.25 for wrong
exams.preparation_tips: MAT is easier than CAT and a good option for Tier-2 and Tier-3 B-schools. Focus on speed — 200 questions in 150 minutes means 45 seconds per question. Use RS Aggarwal for Quant, Word Power Made Easy for English, practice GK from Manorama Yearbook. A score of 600+ (composite out of 800) is good for top MAT-accepting colleges.

exams.for_colleges: XLRI Jamshedpur, XIM Bhubaneswar, XIMB, TAPMI, GIM Goa, IMT Ghaziabad, and 150+ Xavier-associated & other B-Schools
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree from a recognized university with no minimum percentage. Final year students eligible. No age limit.
subjects: Part 1: Quantitative Ability & Data Interpretation (27 questions)
+1 for correct, -0.25 for wrong, additional -0.10 for unattempted questions beyond 8
exams.preparation_tips: XAT is known for its unique Decision Making section — practice ethical dilemmas and business case scenarios. Verbal section is tougher than CAT — read extensively. Note the penalty for unattempted questions — attempt at least 55+ questions. Previous year XAT papers are the best preparation resource. 95+ percentile for XLRI.

exams.for_colleges: AICTE-approved institutions across India. Accepted by JBIMS Mumbai, Goa Institute of Management, SIMSREE, KJ Somaiya, and many state-level B-schools.
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree with minimum 50% (45% for reserved categories). Final year students eligible. No age limit.
subjects: Quantitative Techniques & Data Interpretation (25 questions, 100 marks)
Innovation & Entrepreneurship (25 questions, 100 marks — optional section)
exams.preparation_tips: CMAT is considered easier than CAT/XAT and is a great option for MBA aspirants. Focus on General Awareness — it carries 100 marks and is often the differentiator. Practice Quant and LR from standard books. A score of 280+ out of 400 is excellent. CMAT score valid for JBIMS Mumbai — one of the best ROI B-schools in India.

exams.for_colleges: All CoA-recognized architecture colleges in India (both government and private)
exams.eligibility: 12th pass with Mathematics as compulsory subject. Minimum 50% in PCM aggregate. No age limit.
subjects: Part A (MCQ, 125 marks): Mathematics, General Aptitude, Logical Reasoning, Aesthetic Sensitivity
Part B (Drawing, 75 marks): 2 drawing questions testing creativity, proportion, color sense, 3D perception
exams.preparation_tips: Practice freehand drawing daily — objects, scenes, perspective, 3D compositions. For aptitude: study visual perception, color theory, architectural awareness. Mathematics is Class 12 level — focus on 3D geometry, trigonometry, mensuration. Visit buildings and observe architectural elements. Best of two attempts is considered.

exams.full_name: National Council for Hotel Management & Catering Technology Joint Entrance Exam
exams.conducting_body: National Testing Agency (NTA) on behalf of NCHMCT
exams.for_colleges: 21 Central IHMs (Institute of Hotel Management), 24 State IHMs, and affiliated private hotel management colleges
exams.eligibility: 12th pass from any recognized board with minimum 45% (40% for SC/ST). No specific subject requirement. Age limit: born on or after 01-10-2001 (varies yearly).
uttarakhand_colleges_using: IHM Dehradun (Institute of Hotel Management Dehradun)
exams.preparation_tips: English section carries the most weight — focus on reading comprehension, vocabulary, and grammar. For GK: focus on hospitality industry, tourism, food & culture. Practice aptitude for service sector — questions about hospitality etiquette, food science, and tourism. IHM Dehradun is one of the best central IHMs — rank under 5000 gets admission.

exams.full_name: Union Public Service Commission Civil Services Examination
exams.for_colleges: Lal Bahadur Shastri National Academy of Administration (LBSNAA) Mussoorie for IAS training, National Police Academy Hyderabad for IPS
exams.frequency: Once a year (Prelims: June, Mains: September, Interview: February-April)
exams.eligibility: Indian citizen. Bachelor's degree from recognized university. Age: 21-32 years (General), 21-35 (OBC), 21-37 (SC/ST). Attempts: 6 (General), 9 (OBC), Unlimited (SC/ST) till age limit.
exams.exam_mode: Offline (Prelims: OMR, Mains: Written, Interview: In-person)
subjects: PRELIMS (qualifying): Paper 1 - General Studies (100 MCQs, 200 marks), Paper 2 - CSAT (80 MCQs, 200 marks)
MAINS (merit): 9 Papers including Essay, GS I-IV (250 marks each), Optional Subject (2 papers, 250 each), English & Indian Language (qualifying)
uttarakhand_colleges_using: LBSNAA Mussoorie (IAS Training Academy — located in Uttarakhand!)
exams.preparation_tips: Start with NCERTs (Class 6-12) for all subjects. Read The Hindu / Indian Express daily. Follow Laxmikanth (Polity), Spectrum (Modern History), Shankar IAS (Environment), Indian Economy by Ramesh Singh. Choose optional subject wisely — Geography, Sociology, PSIR are popular. Mussoorie is the IAS training academy — imagine training in your own state! Uttarakhand's reserved seats and local quota through UKPSC is another option.

exams.for_colleges: National Defence Academy (NDA) Khadakwasla, Pune + Indian Military Academy (IMA) Dehradun, Indian Naval Academy (INA) Ezhimala, Air Force Academy (AFA) Dundigal
exams.frequency: Twice a year (NDA I in April, NDA II in September)
exams.eligibility: Male & Female (women allowed from 2024). 12th pass or appearing. Age: 16.5-19.5 years. For Air Force & Navy: 12th with PCM compulsory. Unmarried.
subjects: Paper 2 - General Ability (150 questions, 600 marks, 2.5 hours): English (200), GK (400 - Physics, Chemistry, General Science, History, Geography, Current Affairs)
SSB Interview: 900 marks (5-day process including OIR, PPDT, GD, Interview, GTO)
uttarakhand_colleges_using: Indian Military Academy (IMA) Dehradun — one of the premier military training institutions in Asia
exams.preparation_tips: Start in Class 11. Mathematics: focus on Class 11-12 level (Algebra, Calculus, Trigonometry, Matrices). GAT: Read NCERT Science (Class 9-12), Lucent GK, practice English grammar & comprehension. SSB preparation is equally important — develop leadership, communication, and team skills. IMA Dehradun is right in Uttarakhand — proud tradition! Uttarakhand has a strong military culture, many coaching centers in Dehradun.

for_courses: Officers Training Academy (OTA) - Army (Short Service Commission)
exams.for_colleges: IMA Dehradun, INA Ezhimala, AFA Dundigal, OTA Chennai
exams.frequency: Twice a year (CDS I in April, CDS II in October)
exams.eligibility: Graduation from recognized university. Age: 20-25 years (IMA/AFA), 20-25 (INA), 20-27 (OTA — including women). Unmarried (except OTA women).
subjects: Elementary Mathematics (100 questions, 100 marks, 2 hours) — not required for OTA
exams.preparation_tips: After graduation, CDS is the best route to join armed forces. English: practice comprehension, grammar, vocabulary. GK: focus on History, Polity, Geography, Science, Defence & Current Affairs. Maths: Class 10 level but accuracy matters. Physical fitness is critical for SSB. Many successful CDS officers come from Uttarakhand — the state has a proud military heritage.

exams.full_name: Uttarakhand Public Service Commission Provincial Civil Service Examination
exams.for_colleges: Uttarakhand state government administrative positions — training provided by government
exams.eligibility: Indian citizen and resident of Uttarakhand. Bachelor's degree from recognized university. Age: 21-42 years (General), with relaxation for OBC/SC/ST as per state rules.
exams.exam_mode: Offline (Prelims: OMR, Mains: Written, Interview: In-person)
subjects: PRELIMS: Paper 1 - General Studies (150 questions, 200 marks), Paper 2 - General Aptitude (100 questions, 200 marks — qualifying)
MAINS: 6 Papers — GS I, GS II, GS III (with Uttarakhand specific topics), Hindi, English, Optional Subject
Uttarakhand-specific topics: State History, Culture, Geography, Economy, Char Dham, Gairsain, Chipko Movement
exams.preparation_tips: This is the most important exam for those wanting to serve Uttarakhand directly. Study NCERT + standard UPSC books for general topics. For Uttarakhand-specific: study state's history (Garhwal & Kumaun kingdoms, Tehri Riyasat), geography (Himalayan ecology, glaciers, rivers), culture (Jagar, Pandav Lila, fairs), economy (tourism, horticulture, SIDCUL). Hindi section is mandatory — prepare essay and letter writing. Many coaching centers in Dehradun and Haldwani.

exams.for_colleges: All AICTE-approved pharmacy colleges offering M.Pharm across India. Also used for AICTE scholarships.
exams.eligibility: B.Pharm degree from a recognized university. Final year students also eligible.
exams.preparation_tips: Revise your B.Pharm subjects thoroughly — focus on Pharmacology, Pharmaceutical Chemistry, and Pharmaceutics as they carry the most weight. Use GPAT previous year papers (last 10 years). Standard books: Tripathi (Pharmacology), Beckett & Stenlake (Pharma Chemistry), Lachman (Industrial Pharmacy). GPAT qualifiers get AICTE scholarship of INR 12,400/month during M.Pharm.

exams.full_name: Indian Council of Agricultural Research All India Entrance Examination for Admission
exams.conducting_body: National Testing Agency (NTA) on behalf of ICAR
exams.for_colleges: All ICAR-recognized agricultural universities (including state agricultural universities) and deemed universities
exams.eligibility: 12th pass with PCB or PCM (depending on course) with minimum 50% (40% for SC/ST). Age: maximum 25 years (30 for SC/ST).
uttarakhand_colleges_using: GB Pant University of Agriculture & Technology (Pantnagar) — India's first agricultural university!
VCSG Uttarakhand University of Horticulture & Forestry (Bharsar)
exams.preparation_tips: ICAR AIEEA is similar to NEET in pattern but slightly easier. Focus on NCERT Biology and Chemistry thoroughly. Pantnagar (GB Pant University) is one of India's best agricultural universities and is in Uttarakhand — rank under 3000 can get admission. State quota seats are available. Agriculture is a growing sector with government focus — excellent career stability.

exams.full_name: University Grants Commission National Eligibility Test
exams.conducting_body: National Testing Agency (NTA) on behalf of UGC
exams.for_colleges: All Indian universities and colleges — NET qualification is mandatory for Assistant Professor recruitment
exams.eligibility: Master's degree with minimum 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/OBC-NCL/PwD). Final year PG students also eligible. No age limit for Assistant Professor eligibility. JRF: below 30 years (relaxation for reserved categories).
subjects: Paper 1 (50 questions, 100 marks): Teaching Aptitude, Research Aptitude, Comprehension, Communication, Reasoning, Data Interpretation, IT, Environment, Higher Education
Paper 2 (100 questions, 200 marks): Subject-specific (choose from 83 subjects)
exams.preparation_tips: Paper 1 is common and scoring — master Teaching & Research Aptitude, practice logical reasoning. Paper 2: study your subject's PG syllabus thoroughly. Use Trueman's UGC NET (Paper 1), KVS Madaan. For Paper 2: use subject-specific standard references. JRF cutoff is higher than NET — if you clear JRF, you get INR 37,000/month fellowship for 5 years during PhD. NET is essential for teaching career in Uttarakhand's universities.

exams.for_colleges: Kendriya Vidyalaya (KV), Navodaya Vidyalaya (NVS), Army Schools, DSSSB Schools, and as eligibility for many state government teacher recruitments
exams.eligibility: Paper 1 (PRT): 12th with 50% + D.El.Ed/B.El.Ed/B.Ed. Paper 2 (TGT): Graduation with 50% + B.Ed. Can attempt both papers.
subjects: Paper 1 (Classes 1-5): Child Development & Pedagogy (30), Language I (30), Language II (30), Mathematics (30), Environmental Studies (30)
Paper 2 (Classes 6-8): Child Development & Pedagogy (30), Language I (30), Language II (30), Mathematics & Science OR Social Science (60)
All MCQs, +1 for correct, no negative marking. Minimum 60% (90/150) to qualify.
uttarakhand_colleges_using: Applicable for KV and NVS schools across Uttarakhand
Also useful for UTET (Uttarakhand TET) preparation
exams.preparation_tips: Child Development & Pedagogy (CDP) is the most important section — study Piaget, Vygotsky, Kohlberg, NCF 2005. For languages: practice comprehension passages, grammar, and pedagogy of language teaching. Mathematics/Science: NCERT Class 1-8 level + teaching methodology. CTET is valid for lifetime (since 2021). Combine with UTET for state government school eligibility.

for_courses: Upper Primary Teacher (TGT) - Classes 6-8 in Uttarakhand
exams.for_colleges: All government schools under Uttarakhand state education department
exams.eligibility: Paper 1 (PRT): 12th with 50% + D.El.Ed/B.El.Ed/B.Ed. Paper 2 (TGT): Graduation + B.Ed. Must be a resident of Uttarakhand for state quota.
subjects: Paper 1 (Classes 1-5): Child Development & Pedagogy (30), Language I - Hindi (30), Language II - English (30), Mathematics (30), Environmental Studies (30)
Paper 2 (Classes 6-8): Child Development & Pedagogy (30), Language I (30), Language II (30), Mathematics & Science OR Social Studies (60)
Includes Uttarakhand-specific questions on state geography, culture, and history
uttarakhand_colleges_using: DIET (District Institute of Education & Training) centers across Uttarakhand
exams.preparation_tips: Preparation is similar to CTET but additionally study Uttarakhand-specific content: state's geography (districts, rivers, glaciers), history (Garhwal-Kumaun kingdoms), culture (fairs, festivals, folk dances), and education policies. Study in both Hindi and English as both language papers are mandatory. UTET + government recruitment exam = permanent government teaching job in Uttarakhand.

exams.full_name: Staff Selection Commission Combined Graduate Level Examination
exams.for_colleges: Central Government offices across India — Ministry of Finance, CAG, CBI, NIA, FCI, etc.
exams.frequency: Once a year (Tier 1: April, Tier 2: June/July)
exams.eligibility: Indian citizen. Bachelor's degree from recognized university. Age: 18-32 years (varies by post, with relaxation for OBC/SC/ST).
subjects: Tier 1 (100 questions, 200 marks, 60 min): General Intelligence & Reasoning (25), General Awareness (25), Quantitative Aptitude (25), English Comprehension (25)

Tier 2 (3 papers): Paper 1 — Mathematical Abilities + Reasoning (130 MCQs, 390 marks, 2.5 hrs), Paper 2 — English + General Awareness (105 MCQs + 45 MCQs, 450 marks, 2.5 hrs), Paper 3 — Statistics/JS&LB/Finance (100 MCQs, 200 marks, 2 hrs — for specific posts)
+2 for correct Tier1, +3 for correct Tier2, -0.50 for wrong
uttarakhand_colleges_using: Any recognized university in Uttarakhand — degree from any college is eligible
exams.preparation_tips: SSC CGL is one of the best government job exams for graduates. For Quant: practice Rakesh Yadav or Kiran's. For Reasoning: practice from Arihant or Kiran's. For English: focus on grammar, vocabulary, and comprehension (SP Bakshi). For GK: Lucent GK + Monthly current affairs magazines. Speed is key in Tier 1 (100 questions in 60 minutes). Many coaching centers in Dehradun and Haldwani.

exams.full_name: Birla Institute of Technology and Science Admission Test
exams.for_colleges: BITS Pilani (Pilani campus, Hyderabad campus, Goa campus)
exams.eligibility: 12th pass with PCM with minimum 75% aggregate (and 60% in each PCM subject). Must have passed in one sitting.
subjects: Bonus questions if all 130 attempted (+12 extra questions)
+3 for correct, -1 for wrong, no negative for bonus
exams.preparation_tips: BITSAT is speed-based — 150 questions in 3 hours. Focus on NCERT concepts + JEE Main level problems. Practice speed with previous BITSAT papers and online mock tests. English and Logical Reasoning are unique to BITSAT — prepare separately. BITS Pilani is on par with IITs for placements — average package 15+ LPA. Score of 300+ out of 450 for popular branches.

for_courses: Ground Duty Branch (Administration / Logistics / Education / Meteorology)
exams.for_colleges: Air Force Academy (AFA) Dundigal, Hyderabad — training campus for all IAF officers
exams.eligibility: Indian citizen. Age: 20-24 years (Flying), 20-26 years (Ground Duty/Technical). Graduation: 60% in any degree (Flying/Ground Duty), B.Tech with 60% (Technical Branch). Must be unmarried at time of joining.
subjects: Total: 100 questions, +3 for correct, -1 for wrong
EKT (Engineering Knowledge Test) for Technical Branch: 50 additional questions
uttarakhand_colleges_using: Any recognized Uttarakhand university for graduation
exams.preparation_tips: Focus on General Awareness (especially defence, science, geography, current affairs). English: practice vocabulary, idioms, comprehension. Reasoning: practice from RS Aggarwal. Physical fitness is critical for AFSB. If applying for Flying Branch, ensure you meet medical standards (eyesight, height, weight). Dehradun has excellent coaching for defence exams. Air Force career offers adventure, travel, and prestige.

exams.full_name: Joint Admission Test for M.Sc at IITs
for_courses: M.Sc in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biotechnology, Geology, Statistics
Other post-bachelor programs at IITs & IISc
exams.for_colleges: All IITs (Bombay, Delhi, Kanpur, Madras, Kharagpur, Roorkee, etc.) and IISc Bangalore
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree in relevant science subject with minimum 55% (50% for SC/ST/PwD). Final year students eligible.
subjects: 7 Test Papers: Biotechnology (BT), Chemistry (CY), Economics (EN), Geology (GG), Mathematics (MA), Mathematical Statistics (MS), Physics (PH)
60 questions: 10 MCQs (1 mark each, -1/3 negative), 20 MCQs (2 marks each, -2/3 negative), 10 MSQs (2 marks each, no negative), 20 NATs (1-2 marks, no negative)
exams.preparation_tips: IIT JAM is the best route for B.Sc students to enter IITs. Study from standard reference books for your subject. For Physics: Griffiths, Kleppner. For Chemistry: Atkins, Morrison Boyd. For Maths: Real Analysis by Bartle, Linear Algebra by Friedberg. Practice previous 10 years' papers — pattern is consistent. IIT Roorkee M.Sc programs offer excellent placements and research opportunities. Rank under 500 for IIT Roorkee.

exams.for_colleges: Indira Gandhi National Forest Academy (IGNFA) Dehradun — located in Uttarakhand!
exams.frequency: Once a year (Prelims shared with UPSC CSE in June, Mains in November)
exams.eligibility: Indian citizen. Bachelor's degree with at least one of: Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Science, Botany, Chemistry, Geology, Mathematics, Physics, Statistics, Zoology, Forestry, Agriculture, Engineering. Age: 21-32 years (General), with relaxation for OBC/SC/ST.
exams.exam_mode: Offline (Prelims: OMR shared with CSE, Mains: Written descriptive)
subjects: PRELIMS: Same as UPSC CSE (GS Paper 1 + CSAT Paper 2)
MAINS: General English (300 marks, qualifying), General Knowledge (300 marks), 2 Optional Subject papers (200 marks each x 2 subjects = 800 marks)
Popular optionals: Forestry, Botany, Zoology, Geology, Agriculture
uttarakhand_colleges_using: IGNFA Dehradun (training academy — right in Uttarakhand!)
exams.preparation_tips: This is a dream career for nature lovers — and the training academy (IGNFA) is in Dehradun! Prelims preparation is shared with UPSC CSE. For Mains, choose optionals from your B.Sc background (Botany + Zoology or Forestry + Agriculture are popular combos). Study environment, ecology, biodiversity, and Indian forest policy. Uttarakhand's rich forest cover makes this career especially meaningful. FRI and WII in Dehradun are excellent resources.

exams.full_name: National Institute of Fashion Technology Entrance Exam
for_courses: B.Des (Fashion Design, Textile Design, Accessory Design, Knitwear Design, Leather Design)
exams.for_colleges: 18 NIFT campuses across India (New Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Kolkata, Hyderabad, etc.)
exams.eligibility: UG: 12th pass from any recognized board. No minimum percentage. PG: relevant graduation. No age limit.
subjects: GAT (General Ability Test): Quantitative Ability, Communication Ability, English Comprehension, Analytical & Logical Ability, GK, Current Affairs (150 questions)
CAT (Creative Ability Test) for B.Des: Creativity, design sense, color, innovation (hands-on, 3 hours)
Situation Test: Make a 3D model from given materials (for B.Des shortlisted candidates)
exams.preparation_tips: For CAT: practice sketching, color compositions, and creative thinking daily. Study design elements (line, shape, texture, color theory). For GAT: standard aptitude preparation + GK. Situation Test: practice making 3D models from paper, clay, and mixed materials. Study fashion industry trends, designers, and fashion history. NIFT graduates are highly sought after — placements of 8-15 LPA.

exams.conducting_body: Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI)
for_courses: CA Foundation → CA Intermediate → CA Final = Chartered Accountant
exams.for_colleges: ICAI (self-study + articleship with practicing CA firms). Not a college-based course.
exams.eligibility: 12th pass from any recognized board. Registration with ICAI must be done at least 4 months before the exam.
subjects: Paper 1: Accounting (100 marks, 3 hours) — Subjective + Objective
Paper 2: Business Laws & Business Correspondence (100 marks, 3 hours) — Subjective + Objective
Paper 3: Business Mathematics, Statistics & Logical Reasoning (100 marks, 2 hours) — Objective
Paper 4: Business Economics & Business & Commercial Knowledge (100 marks, 2 hours) — Objective
uttarakhand_colleges_using: CA coaching available in Dehradun, Haldwani, Haridwar
B.Com from any Uttarakhand university is a good base alongside CA
exams.preparation_tips: Register with ICAI immediately after 12th. Accounting and Business Laws require thorough study of ICAI study material. For Maths: practice calculation speed. Start CA coaching alongside B.Com for best results. CA Foundation pass rate is around 20-25% — don't take it lightly. ICAI study material is sufficient but coaching helps. Many successful CAs from Uttarakhand practice in Dehradun, Delhi-NCR.

also_known_as: Institute of Banking Personnel Selection Probationary Officer
exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline from a recognized university. Age: 20-30 years (relaxation for reserved categories).
exams.exam_pattern: Prelims: 100 marks, 1 hour (English, Quant, Reasoning 30+35+35 Qs). Mains: 200 marks, 3 hours (Reasoning & Computer Aptitude, English, Data Analysis, General/Economy/Banking Awareness). Interview: 100 marks.
exams.important_dates: Notification: August. Prelims: October. Mains: November. Interview: January-February.
preparation_tips: Master quantitative aptitude and reasoning basics first
Read newspapers daily for current affairs and banking awareness
Practice previous year papers under timed conditions
Focus on speed and accuracy for prelims
exams.what_it_leads_to: Appointment as Probationary Officer in 11 participating public sector banks across India.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Multiple public sector bank branches in Uttarakhand recruit POs through IBPS. Good opportunity for candidates from hill districts.

exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline from a recognized university. Age: 20-28 years (relaxation for reserved categories).
exams.exam_pattern: Prelims: 100 marks, 1 hour (English 30, Numerical Ability 35, Reasoning 35). Mains: 200 marks, 2 hours 40 minutes (General/Financial Awareness, English, Reasoning & Computer Aptitude, Quantitative Aptitude).
exams.important_dates: Notification: July-August. Prelims: August-September. Mains: October.
preparation_tips: Focus on speed since time is very limited
Practice sectional mock tests regularly
Build strong basics in arithmetic and reasoning
Read banking awareness capsules before mains
exams.what_it_leads_to: Appointment as Clerk in 11 participating public sector banks. Career progression to officer cadre possible.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Clerk postings available in Uttarakhand branches of public sector banks. Suitable for candidates seeking stable government banking jobs in the state.

also_known_as: State Bank of India Probationary Officer
exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline from a recognized university. Age: 21-30 years (relaxation for reserved categories).
exams.exam_pattern: Prelims: 100 marks, 1 hour (English 30, Quant 35, Reasoning 35). Mains: 250 marks, 3 hours (Reasoning & Computer Aptitude 60, Data Analysis 60, General/Economy/Banking 40, English 40, Descriptive English 50). Group Exercises & Interview.
exams.important_dates: Notification: March-April. Prelims: June. Mains: July. Interview: September-October.
preparation_tips: Focus on descriptive English writing for mains
Prepare case studies for group exercises
Practice data interpretation extensively
Stay updated on RBI policies and banking reforms
exams.what_it_leads_to: Appointment as Probationary Officer in SBI, India's largest bank. Excellent career growth to managerial positions.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: SBI has extensive branch network across Uttarakhand including remote hill areas. POs may be posted in Uttarakhand branches.

exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline from a recognized university. Age: 20-28 years (relaxation for reserved categories).
exams.exam_pattern: Prelims: 100 marks, 1 hour (English 30, Numerical Ability 35, Reasoning 35). Mains: 200 marks, 2 hours 40 minutes (General/Financial Awareness 50, General English 40, Quantitative Aptitude 50, Reasoning & Computer Aptitude 60).
exams.important_dates: Notification: November-December. Prelims: January-February. Mains: March-April.
preparation_tips: Time management is critical — practice with a timer
Focus on high-scoring topics in quant and reasoning
Build vocabulary and grammar for English section
Study financial awareness thoroughly for mains
exams.what_it_leads_to: Appointment as Junior Associate (Customer Support & Sales) in SBI. Promotion path to officer cadre available.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: SBI Clerk postings available across Uttarakhand. State-specific vacancies are often announced for Uttarakhand circle.

also_known_as: Reserve Bank of India Grade B Officer
exams.eligibility: Minimum 60% in graduation (50% for SC/ST/PwBD). Age: 21-30 years. Master's degree preferred but not mandatory.
exams.exam_pattern: Phase 1: 200 marks, 2 hours (General Awareness 80, Quantitative Aptitude 30, English 30, Reasoning 60). Phase 2: 300 marks, 3 hours (Economic & Social Issues 100, Finance & Management 100, Descriptive English 100). Interview.
exams.important_dates: Notification: March-April. Phase 1: May-June. Phase 2: July-August. Interview: September-October.
preparation_tips: Study economics and finance in depth — ESI paper is crucial
Read Economic Survey and RBI Annual Report
Practice essay writing for descriptive English
Follow RBI monetary policy updates regularly
exams.what_it_leads_to: Appointment as Grade B Officer (Manager level) in RBI. One of the most prestigious banking positions in India with excellent growth to Executive Director level.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: RBI has offices in Dehradun. Limited but prestigious postings available in the state.

also_known_as: Railway Recruitment Board Non-Technical Popular Categories
exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline for some posts, Class 12 pass for others. Age: 18-33 years (varies by post, relaxation for reserved categories).
exams.exam_pattern: CBT 1: 100 marks, 90 minutes (General Awareness 40, Mathematics 30, General Intelligence & Reasoning 30). CBT 2: 120 marks, 90 minutes (General Awareness 50, Mathematics 35, General Intelligence & Reasoning 35). Typing Skill Test/CBAT for specific posts.
exams.important_dates: Varies by recruitment cycle. Notification typically followed by 3-4 month preparation window.
preparation_tips: Focus on general awareness — very high weightage
Practice arithmetic and number series for maths
Solve reasoning puzzles daily
Study railway-specific GK for CBT 2
exams.what_it_leads_to: Various non-technical posts in Indian Railways including Junior Clerk cum Typist, Accounts Clerk cum Typist, Commercial cum Ticket Clerk, Traffic Assistant, Station Master, Goods Guard.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Railway stations across Uttarakhand (Dehradun, Haridwar, Roorkee, Kathgodam, Ramnagar) require NTPC staff. Good opportunity for local candidates.

exams.eligibility: Diploma or B.E./B.Tech in relevant engineering discipline. Age: 18-33 years (relaxation for reserved categories).
exams.exam_pattern: CBT 1: 100 marks, 90 minutes (Mathematics 30, General Intelligence & Reasoning 25, General Awareness 15, General Science 30). CBT 2: 150 marks, 120 minutes (Technical abilities based on branch). Document Verification.
exams.important_dates: Varies by recruitment cycle. CBT 1 typically held 3-4 months after notification.
preparation_tips: Focus on technical subjects of your engineering branch
Revise diploma/degree-level concepts thoroughly
Practice General Science especially Physics
Solve previous year RRB JE papers
exams.what_it_leads_to: Appointment as Junior Engineer in Indian Railways across departments like Electrical, Mechanical, Civil, Electronics, IT.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Railway infrastructure projects in Uttarakhand (Rishikesh-Karnaprayag rail line) create JE vacancies. Northern Railway zone covers the state.

exams.eligibility: 10th pass (Matriculation) from a recognized board. ITI certification preferred. Age: 18-33 years (relaxation for reserved categories).
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 100 marks, 90 minutes (Mathematics 25, General Intelligence & Reasoning 30, General Science 25, General Awareness & Current Affairs 20). Physical Efficiency Test (PET). Document Verification.
exams.frequency: Based on vacancies (typically every 3-4 years with large-scale recruitment)
exams.important_dates: Varies by recruitment cycle. Notification typically 4-6 months before exam.
preparation_tips: Focus on basic arithmetic, ratio, percentage for maths
Study NCERT Science books for Class 9-10
Practice reasoning puzzles and coding-decoding
Stay updated on current affairs for last 6 months
exams.what_it_leads_to: Posts such as Track Maintainer, Helper, Assistant Pointsman in Indian Railways. Entry-level railway positions with promotion opportunities.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Track maintenance and helper positions available at Uttarakhand railway stations. Accessible opportunity for Class 10 pass candidates from the state.

exams.eligibility: Diploma or B.E./B.Tech in Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, or Quantity Surveying & Contracts. Age: up to 32 years (varies by department).
exams.exam_pattern: Paper 1 (CBT): 200 marks, 2 hours (General Intelligence & Reasoning 50, General Awareness 50, General Engineering 100). Paper 2 (Descriptive): 300 marks, 2 hours (Technical questions from respective discipline).
exams.important_dates: Notification: September-October. Paper 1: November-December. Paper 2: March. Result: June-July.
preparation_tips: Focus heavily on General Engineering — it carries maximum weightage
Revise core engineering subjects from diploma/degree
Practice previous year SSC JE papers thoroughly
Don't neglect GK and reasoning as they decide cutoff
exams.what_it_leads_to: Junior Engineer posts in CPWD, CWC, MES, BRO, and other central government departments. Good salary and career growth to Assistant Engineer.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: BRO (Border Roads Organisation) and CPWD have significant projects in Uttarakhand. JE postings in the state are common for infrastructure projects.

exams.conducting_body: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development)
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree with minimum 60% (50% for SC/ST) for RDBS. For DEPR: Master's in Economics with 60%. Age: 21-30 years.
exams.exam_pattern: Prelims: 200 marks, 2 hours (Quantitative Aptitude 20, Reasoning 20, English 40, General Awareness 20, Computer Knowledge 20, Agriculture & Rural Development 40). Mains: 200 marks, 2 hours (ESI, Agriculture & Rural Development, Descriptive English). Interview: 50 marks.
exams.important_dates: Notification: July-August. Prelims: September. Mains: October-November. Interview: January.
preparation_tips: Study agriculture and rural development in depth
Read NABARD Annual Report and Economic Survey
Focus on schemes related to rural India, SHGs, microfinance
Practice descriptive writing on economic topics
exams.what_it_leads_to: Appointment as Assistant Manager (Grade A) in NABARD. Prestigious role in agricultural and rural development finance with excellent career growth.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: NABARD has a regional office in Dehradun and plays a key role in financing agriculture and rural development in Uttarakhand hill regions.

exams.eligibility: Unmarried female candidates, Class 12 pass with Physics, Chemistry, Biology with 50% aggregate and English. Age: 17-25 years.
exams.exam_pattern: Written Test: 150 marks, 90 minutes (General English, Biology, Physics, Chemistry, General Intelligence). Interview and medical fitness test.
exams.important_dates: Notification: December-January. Exam: March-April. Result: May-June. Admission: July.
preparation_tips: Focus on NCERT Biology and Chemistry for Class 11-12
Practice English grammar and vocabulary
Study general intelligence and reasoning
Maintain physical fitness for medical examination
exams.what_it_leads_to: B.Sc Nursing at Armed Forces Medical Services institutions. Commission as Lieutenant in Indian Army upon completion. Prestigious defence nursing career.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Military hospitals in Uttarakhand (Dehradun, Roorkee) may have MNS postings. Defence culture in Uttarakhand makes it a popular career choice.

exams.conducting_body: AIIMS (All India Institute of Medical Sciences), New Delhi
exams.eligibility: Class 12 pass with Science stream (PCB) with 60% aggregate (55% for SC/ST). Age: 17-25 years.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 70-80 MCQs, 90 minutes. Subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Biology, General Knowledge & Aptitude. Negative marking for wrong answers.
exams.important_dates: Notification: January-February. Exam: May-June. Counselling: July.
preparation_tips: Study NCERT Biology, Physics, Chemistry thoroughly
Practice MCQs from previous AIIMS nursing papers
Focus on human anatomy and physiology topics
Keep updated on general health and science awareness
exams.what_it_leads_to: B.Sc Nursing at AIIMS institutions across India. Highly valued nursing degree with excellent clinical training and career prospects.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: AIIMS Rishikesh offers nursing programs. Direct relevance for Uttarakhand students seeking top-tier nursing education in the state.

also_known_as: National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Master of Dental Surgery
exams.conducting_body: National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences (NBEMS)
exams.eligibility: BDS degree from a recognized university with completion of 1-year compulsory rotatory internship. Must be registered with State Dental Council or DCI.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 240 MCQs, 3 hours. Covers all dental subjects from BDS curriculum. Each correct answer: 4 marks. Negative marking: -1.
exams.important_dates: Notification: September-October. Exam: January-March. Counselling: March-April (AIQ) and state counselling subsequently.
preparation_tips: Revise all BDS subjects systematically
Focus on high-yield topics: Oral Surgery, Orthodontics, Prosthodontics
Practice previous year NEET MDS papers extensively
Use standard dental textbooks for concept clarity
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to MDS programs in government and private dental colleges across India. Specialization in various dental disciplines.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Dental colleges in Uttarakhand (Dehradun, Roorkee) offer MDS seats through NEET MDS counselling. State quota seats available.

also_known_as: National Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research Joint Entrance Exam
exams.conducting_body: NIPER (National Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research)
exams.eligibility: B.Pharm/M.Pharm/B.Tech (Pharmaceutical) or equivalent with minimum 60% (55% for SC/ST). GPAT qualified preferred.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 200 MCQs, 2 hours. Subjects vary by course: Pharmaceutical Sciences, Pharmacology, Medicinal Chemistry, etc. No negative marking.
exams.important_dates: Notification: April-May. Exam: June-July. Counselling: August.
preparation_tips: Focus on core pharmaceutical subjects from B.Pharm curriculum
Study previous year NIPER JEE papers for pattern understanding
Revise pharmacology, medicinal chemistry, pharmaceutics thoroughly
Clear GPAT first for better preparation baseline
exams.what_it_leads_to: M.S. (Pharm), M.Pharm, MBA (Pharma), and Ph.D programs at 7 NIPERs across India. Premier pharmaceutical education.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Pharmaceutical companies and research in Uttarakhand (Selaqui pharma belt near Dehradun) benefit from NIPER graduates. No NIPER campus in Uttarakhand but graduates find local employment.

exams.conducting_body: AIIMS (All India Institute of Medical Sciences)
exams.eligibility: Class 12 pass with Science stream (PCB) for most courses. Specific eligibility varies by paramedical course. Age: 17-25 years typically.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 100 MCQs, 90 minutes. Subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Biology, General Knowledge. Some courses may have additional aptitude components.
exams.important_dates: Notification: March-April. Exam: June-July. Counselling: August.
preparation_tips: Focus on NCERT Science textbooks for Class 11-12
Study previous year AIIMS paramedical papers
Understand basics of anatomy and physiology
Keep updated on health sector current affairs
exams.what_it_leads_to: Various paramedical programs at AIIMS including B.Sc in Medical Technology (Radiography, Lab Technology), Operation Theatre Technology, Optometry, etc.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: AIIMS Rishikesh offers multiple paramedical courses. Excellent opportunity for Uttarakhand students to get premier healthcare training locally.

exams.eligibility: For BA LLB (5-year): Class 12 pass with 50% marks. For LLM: LLB/BA LLB with 50%. For Ph.D: LLM with 55%. Age: No upper limit for BA LLB.
exams.exam_pattern: Offline exam: 150 marks, 1.5 hours (English 25, Current Affairs & GK 35, Legal Aptitude 35, Logical Reasoning 35, Mathematics 20). No negative marking.
exams.important_dates: Notification: September. Application deadline: November-January. Exam: June (before CLAT). Result: June.
preparation_tips: Focus on legal reasoning and legal aptitude questions
Read The Hindu/Indian Express daily for current affairs
Practice logical reasoning and analytical skills
Solve previous year AILET papers for pattern familiarity
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to NLU Delhi for BA LLB, LLM, and Ph.D programs. NLU Delhi is among the top 3 law universities in India.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: No direct Uttarakhand campus, but Uttarakhand students aspiring for top NLUs should consider AILET alongside CLAT.

exams.conducting_body: Pearson VUE (under license from Law School Admission Council, USA)
exams.eligibility: No specific eligibility criteria. Open to anyone seeking admission to participating law schools. Class 12 pass/appearing for UG law.
exams.exam_pattern: Online proctored exam: 92-100 MCQs, 2 hours 20 minutes. Sections: Analytical Reasoning, Logical Reasoning (2 sections), Reading Comprehension. No negative marking.
exams.frequency: Multiple sessions per year (typically January, May-June)
exams.important_dates: Registration opens several months before each session. Scores valid for 1 year.
preparation_tips: Practice analytical reasoning (logic games) extensively
Build reading speed and comprehension for long passages
Focus on strengthening/weakening argument questions
Take multiple full-length practice tests
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to 100+ law colleges in India including Jindal Global Law School, UPES Dehradun Law, and other private law universities.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: UPES Dehradun accepts LSAT India scores for its law programs. Direct option for Uttarakhand-based law aspirants.

also_known_as: Maharashtra Common Entrance Test for Law
exams.conducting_body: State Common Entrance Test Cell, Government of Maharashtra
exams.eligibility: For 5-year LLB: Class 12 pass with 45% (40% for reserved). For 3-year LLB: Graduate with 45% (40% for reserved). Maharashtra domicile for state quota.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 150 MCQs, 2 hours. Subjects: Legal Aptitude & Legal Reasoning, Logical & Analytical Reasoning, General Knowledge & Current Affairs, English. No negative marking.
exams.important_dates: Notification: December-January. Exam: May-June. Counselling: July-August.
preparation_tips: Focus on legal aptitude questions based on legal principles
Practice reading comprehension and analytical reasoning
Study Maharashtra-specific current affairs
Solve previous year MH CET Law papers
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to government and private law colleges in Maharashtra including Government Law College Mumbai, ILS Pune, and other premier institutes.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Primarily for Maharashtra law colleges. Limited relevance for Uttarakhand unless students wish to study law in Maharashtra.

exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline with minimum 50% aggregate. Final year students may also apply.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 120 questions, 2 hours. Sections: Language Skills (36 Qs, 28 min), Quantitative Skills (36 Qs, 52 min), Logical Reasoning (36 Qs, 40 min). Scored on 12-360 scale. No negative marking.
exams.frequency: Multiple windows (October to December, with option to retake up to 3 times)
exams.important_dates: Registration: August-October. Test Window: October-December. Results: Within 4-5 weeks of test.
preparation_tips: Practice time management — each section is individually timed
Focus on language skills for quick scoring
Build strong fundamentals in data interpretation
Take all 3 attempts to maximize your score
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to NMIMS Mumbai MBA, NMIMS campuses, and 40+ partner B-schools. Also accepted by ISB, XIMB, and other institutes.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Limited direct relevance to Uttarakhand, but NMAT score is accepted by multiple B-schools where Uttarakhand students may apply.

exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline with minimum 50% marks (45% for SC/ST). Must register for Symbiosis Entrance Test (SET) as well.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 60 MCQs, 60 minutes. Sections: General English (15 Qs), Quantitative Aptitude, Data Interpretation & Data Sufficiency (20 Qs), Analytical & Logical Reasoning (25 Qs). Marking: +1 for correct, -0.25 for wrong.
exams.frequency: Once a year (3 test attempts in December)
exams.important_dates: Registration: August-November. Exam: December (3 attempts). Results: January. GE-PIWAT: February-March.
preparation_tips: Speed is crucial — only 60 minutes for 60 questions
Focus on logical reasoning for maximum questions
Practice quick reading and vocabulary for English
Take all 3 attempts and best score is considered
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to MBA programs at 16 Symbiosis institutes including SIBM Pune, SCMHRD, SIIB, SIMS, SITM, etc.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: No Symbiosis campus in Uttarakhand, but popular among Uttarakhand MBA aspirants seeking quality private B-school education.

exams.eligibility: No specific academic eligibility. Open to anyone. Must be 18+ years. Accepted globally for MBA/business programs.
exams.exam_pattern: GMAT Focus Edition (2023+): 64 questions, 2 hours 15 minutes. Three sections: Quantitative Reasoning (21 Qs, 45 min), Verbal Reasoning (23 Qs, 45 min), Data Insights (20 Qs, 45 min). Score range: 205-805.
exams.frequency: Year-round at test centers. Can take up to 5 times in a 12-month period.
exams.important_dates: Available year-round. Register 24 hours before test date. Score valid for 5 years.
preparation_tips: Start with official GMAT prep materials from GMAC
Focus on Data Insights — new section requires specific practice
Build strong verbal reasoning skills for critical reasoning
Take official mock tests to gauge readiness
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to top MBA programs globally (Harvard, Wharton, INSEAD, ISB) and in India (IIMs, ISB, XLRI). Essential for international MBA applications.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Test centers available in Delhi/NCR. Relevant for Uttarakhand students aspiring for international or top Indian MBA programs.

also_known_as: Undergraduate Common Entrance Exam for Design
exams.conducting_body: IIT Bombay (in collaboration with IIT Delhi, IIT Guwahati, IIITDM Jabalpur)
exams.eligibility: Class 12 pass/appearing. Must have been born on or after October 1 of the year 20 years before the exam year. Maximum 3 attempts.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 3 hours. Part A: NAT (Numerical Answer Type) + MSQ (Multiple Select Questions). Part B: Sketching (on paper). Total 300 marks. Tests creativity, visualization, observation, design aptitude.
exams.important_dates: Registration: October-November. Exam: January. Results: March. Counselling: June (through JoSAA).
preparation_tips: Practice sketching and visual communication daily
Develop observation skills through everyday design analysis
Study color theory, typography, and basic design principles
Take mock tests for Part A numerical and analytical questions
exams.what_it_leads_to: B.Des programs at IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, IIT Guwahati, IIT Hyderabad, and IIITDM Jabalpur. Premier design education in India.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: No direct Uttarakhand campus, but relevant for design-aspiring students from the state. Counselling through JoSAA.

exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree in any discipline (for M.Des at IITs/IISc). No age limit. Maximum 3 attempts.
exams.exam_pattern: Part A (CBT): 100 marks, 3 hours — NAT, MSQ, MCQ testing design aptitude, visualization, logical reasoning. Part B (offline): 100 marks, 1 hour — sketching and drawing on paper.
exams.important_dates: Registration: October-November. Exam: January. Results: March. Counselling: May-June.
preparation_tips: Build a strong design portfolio for interviews
Practice sketching product ideas and concepts daily
Study design history, famous designers, and design movements
Develop visual reasoning and spatial aptitude
exams.what_it_leads_to: M.Des programs at IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, IIT Guwahati, IIT Hyderabad, IIT Kanpur, IISc Bangalore, and other design institutes.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Limited direct relevance to Uttarakhand, but postgraduate design education option for graduates from the state.

also_known_as: National Institute of Design Design Aptitude Test
exams.eligibility: For B.Des (DAT Prelims): Class 12 pass/appearing in any stream. For M.Des (DAT Mains): Bachelor's degree in relevant discipline. No age limit.
exams.exam_pattern: DAT Prelims (B.Des): Objective MCQs on design aptitude, creativity, general knowledge. DAT Mains: Studio test (sketching, model making, design problem solving), Interview/Portfolio presentation. Total assessment over 2 stages.
exams.important_dates: Registration: October-December. DAT Prelims: January. DAT Mains: April-May. Results: June.
preparation_tips: Develop strong sketching and visualization skills
Study Indian art, craft, and cultural heritage
Practice creative problem solving and lateral thinking
Build observation skills — NID tests real-world awareness
exams.what_it_leads_to: B.Des and M.Des programs at NID campuses (Ahmedabad, Gandhinagar, Bangalore, Kurukshetra, Vijayawada, Bhopal, Jorhat). NID is India's premier design institute.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: No NID campus in Uttarakhand, but Uttarakhand's rich craft traditions (Aipan art, woodcraft) can be a unique portfolio strength for applicants.

exams.eligibility: Class 12 pass/appearing with 50% aggregate. For B.Arch: should have Mathematics as a subject. For B.Planning: Maths compulsory.
exams.exam_pattern: Paper 2A (B.Arch): 400 marks, 3 hours — Mathematics (MCQ 20+NAT 5), Aptitude Test (MCQ 50), Drawing Test (2 Qs). Paper 2B (B.Planning): 400 marks, 3 hours — Mathematics (MCQ 20+NAT 5), Aptitude Test (MCQ 50), Planning Based Questions (MCQ 25).
exams.important_dates: Registration: February. Exam: April. Results: May. Counselling via JoSAA: June-July.
preparation_tips: Practice architectural drawing and 3D visualization daily
Study aptitude section — covers GK, architecture, design
Strengthen mathematics for both papers
Solve previous year drawing questions under timed conditions
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to B.Arch at NITs, IITs (through AAT), SPAs, and other government architecture colleges. B.Planning at top planning institutes through JoSAA counselling.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Relevant for admission to architecture programs. NIT Uttarakhand and other Uttarakhand colleges may offer B.Arch/B.Planning through this score.

exams.eligibility: B.E./B.Tech for technical branches. Any degree for executive/education branches. Unmarried, age 19-25 years. Must meet physical and medical standards.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 4 sections (English, Reasoning & Numerical Ability, General Science, Mathematical Aptitude), 2 hours. Each section has qualifying cutoff. Followed by SSB Interview.
exams.important_dates: Notification: June and December. Exam: September and March. SSB: 3-4 months after result.
preparation_tips: Focus on general science with emphasis on physics and mechanics
Practice numerical ability with focus on speed
Build strong English comprehension and vocabulary
Prepare for SSB concurrently — start early
exams.what_it_leads_to: Commissioned officer entry into Indian Navy across branches: Executive, Engineering, Electrical, Education, Logistics.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Uttarakhand has a strong defence services tradition. Though landlocked, many Uttarakhand youth join the Navy through INET.

exams.conducting_body: Services Selection Board (under Ministry of Defence)
exams.eligibility: Must have qualified the written exam (CDS/NDA/AFCAT/INET/CES etc.) or be shortlisted by the respective defence entry scheme.
exams.exam_pattern: 5-day process: Day 1 — Screening (OIR Test + PPDT). Days 2-3 — Psychological Tests (TAT, WAT, SRT, Self Description). Day 4 — Group Testing (GD, GPE, Lecturette, HGT, Individual & Command Tasks). Day 5 — Personal Interview by IO. Conference.
exams.frequency: Continuous throughout the year for various entries
exams.important_dates: Scheduled individually after clearing the respective written exam. Dates communicated via call letter.
preparation_tips: Develop genuine Officer Like Qualities (OLQs)
Practice story writing (TAT) and word association (WAT) regularly
Work on communication skills and body language
Build real-life experience in leadership, teamwork, and adventure
exams.what_it_leads_to: Final selection for commissioning as officer in Indian Army, Navy, or Air Force. The SSB is the decisive selection stage.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: SSB centers exist in Dehradun (for Army), Allahabad, Bhopal, and Bangalore. Dehradun SSB center is a major one. Uttarakhand's defence culture gives local candidates an advantage.

exams.conducting_body: Directorate General of Territorial Army (Indian Army)
exams.eligibility: Employed (gainfully) Indian citizens, both male and female. Age: 18-42 years. Graduate in any discipline. Must be medically fit.
exams.exam_pattern: Written Exam: Paper 1 — Reasoning & Elementary Mathematics (100 marks, 2 hours). Paper 2 — General Knowledge & English (100 marks, 2 hours). Followed by SSB Interview and Medical.
exams.frequency: Twice a year (typically July and January)
exams.important_dates: Notification: February and August. Exam: July and January. SSB: 3-4 months after written result.
preparation_tips: Focus on elementary math — topics up to Class 10 level
Build strong English language skills
Study current affairs and defence-related GK
Prepare for SSB interview alongside written preparation
exams.what_it_leads_to: Commission as officer in the Territorial Army. Part-time military service while maintaining civilian career. Two months annual training.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: TA battalions exist in Uttarakhand (Kumaon and Garhwal). Popular among employed youth in the state who want to serve part-time in the military.

also_known_as: Indian Institute of Mass Communication Entrance Test
exams.conducting_body: Indian Institute of Mass Communication (IIMC), New Delhi
exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline with minimum 50% marks (45% for reserved categories). For Hindi Journalism: Hindi as subject in graduation/Class 12.
exams.exam_pattern: Written Test: 100 MCQs on General Knowledge, Current Affairs, English/Hindi Language, Media Aptitude, Analytical Ability. Duration: 2 hours. Followed by Group Discussion and Personal Interview.
exams.important_dates: Notification: May-June. Exam: July. GD/PI: August. Admission: August-September.
preparation_tips: Read newspapers and magazines extensively for current affairs
Develop strong opinions on media ethics and contemporary issues
Practice writing on media, communication, and social issues
Prepare for GD/PI with strong articulation of views
exams.what_it_leads_to: PG Diploma in Journalism (English/Hindi), Advertising & PR, Radio & TV Journalism, New Media at IIMC campuses. Premier media education in India.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: IIMC has no Uttarakhand campus, but media and journalism opportunities in the state are growing. IIMC graduates can work in Uttarakhand's expanding media landscape.

also_known_as: Film and Television Institute of India Entrance Test
exams.conducting_body: Film and Television Institute of India (FTII), Pune
exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline (for most courses). Specific courses may require relevant background. Age: No upper limit for most courses.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: MCQs on General Knowledge, Current Affairs, Analytical Ability, and course-specific aptitude. Followed by screen test/interview depending on specialization (Direction, Cinematography, Sound, Editing, etc.).
exams.frequency: Once a year (or every 2 years for some courses)
exams.important_dates: Notification: January-March. Written Test: April-May. Orientation/Interview: June-July. Admission: August.
preparation_tips: Watch classic and contemporary Indian and world cinema extensively
Study film history, theory, and major film movements
Develop analytical skills to critique films and narratives
Build a portfolio of creative work (shorts, photography, writing)
exams.what_it_leads_to: PG Diploma in Film Direction, Cinematography, Sound Design, Editing, Screenplay Writing, Acting, Art Direction at India's premier film school.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Uttarakhand is increasingly used as a film shooting location. FTII graduates can leverage the state's natural beauty for film production careers.

also_known_as: National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences Entrance Exam
exams.conducting_body: NIMHANS (National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences), Bangalore
exams.eligibility: Varies by program. For M.Phil Clinical Psychology: MA/M.Sc in Psychology with 55%. For DM/M.Ch: MD/MS in relevant specialty. For Ph.D: M.Phil/MD in relevant field.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: MCQs on core subject areas. For Clinical Psychology: Psychopathology, Clinical Psychology, Research Methods, Statistics, Psychological Assessment, Therapeutic Approaches. Duration varies by program.
exams.important_dates: Notification: March-April. Exam: May-June. Results: June-July. Admission: July-August.
preparation_tips: Study abnormal psychology and psychopathology in depth
Revise research methodology and statistics
Study DSM-5 and ICD-11 classification systems
Read published research papers from NIMHANS faculty
exams.what_it_leads_to: M.Phil (Clinical Psychology, Psychiatric Social Work), DM (Neurology, Psychiatry), M.Ch (Neurosurgery), Ph.D, and Fellowship programs at India's premier mental health institute.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Mental health services are underserved in Uttarakhand hill regions. NIMHANS graduates can fill a critical gap in the state's mental health infrastructure.

exams.eligibility: Master's degree in Agriculture/Veterinary/Fisheries/Home Science/relevant subject with minimum 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD).
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: 150 MCQs, 2 hours 30 minutes. Subjects: Core discipline (unit-wise). Qualifying for NET (Lecturership) and JRF/SRF (Research Fellowship). Negative marking: -1/3 for wrong answers.
exams.important_dates: Notification: April-May. Exam: August-September. Results: October-November.
preparation_tips: Study subject-specific topics from ICAR syllabus thoroughly
Revise MSc-level concepts in your specialization
Practice previous year ICAR NET papers
Focus on recent advances in agricultural sciences
exams.what_it_leads_to: NET: Eligibility for Assistant Professor in agricultural universities. JRF/SRF: Research fellowship for Ph.D in ICAR institutes and agricultural universities.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: GBPUAT Pantnagar, VCSG Uttarakhand University of Horticulture & Forestry, and other agricultural institutions in Uttarakhand recruit faculty through ICAR NET qualification.

also_known_as: Wildlife Institute of India Entrance Exam
exams.eligibility: Varies by program. For M.Sc Wildlife Science: B.Sc in Life Sciences/Forestry with 55%. For PG Diploma: Graduate with relevant experience. For Ph.D: M.Sc in relevant discipline.
exams.exam_pattern: Written Test: MCQs on Wildlife Biology, Ecology, Conservation Biology, Environmental Science, General Science, Current Affairs related to wildlife. Followed by Interview.
exams.important_dates: Notification: January-March. Written Test: April-May. Interview: June. Admission: July.
preparation_tips: Study wildlife biology, ecology, and conservation biology thoroughly
Read current affairs on wildlife and environment issues
Understand Indian Wildlife Protection Act and CITES
Gain field experience through wildlife volunteering
exams.what_it_leads_to: M.Sc Wildlife Science, PG Diploma in Wildlife Management, Ph.D at WII. Careers in wildlife conservation, forest services, environmental consulting.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: WII is located in Dehradun, Uttarakhand. The state has rich biodiversity (Jim Corbett, Rajaji, Valley of Flowers, Nanda Devi). Highly relevant for Uttarakhand's conservation and eco-tourism sector.

also_known_as: National Institute of Sports Entrance Exam
exams.conducting_body: Sports Authority of India (SAI), NIS Patiala
exams.eligibility: Graduate with sports background. For Diploma in Sports Coaching: Represented at state/national level in respective sport. Age and experience requirements vary by course.
exams.exam_pattern: Written Test: General Knowledge, Sports Science, Physical Education concepts. Physical Fitness Test. Sport-specific skill assessment. Interview/Viva voce.
exams.important_dates: Notification: March-April. Exam: May-June. Admission: July-August.
preparation_tips: Maintain excellent physical fitness levels
Study sports science — physiology, biomechanics, nutrition
Know rules and regulations of your specific sport
Study history of sports in India and Olympics
exams.what_it_leads_to: Diploma in Sports Coaching, M.Sc in Sports Coaching, and Certificate courses at NIS Patiala. Career as certified sports coach under SAI.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Uttarakhand has produced national-level athletes in adventure sports, athletics. NIS qualification benefits aspiring coaches in the state. State Sports Authority jobs often prefer NIS certification.

exams.conducting_body: NTA (National Testing Agency) on behalf of UGC
exams.eligibility: Master's degree in Physical Education with minimum 55% marks (50% for reserved categories). Final year students may also apply.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: Paper 1 (50 Qs, 100 marks) — Teaching Aptitude, Research Methodology, Communication, etc. Paper 2 (100 Qs, 200 marks) — Physical Education subject-specific. Total 3 hours. Negative marking: None.
exams.important_dates: June session: Registration March-April, Exam June. December session: Registration September-October, Exam December.
preparation_tips: Study physical education pedagogy and methodology
Revise sports psychology, kinesiology, biomechanics
Practice Paper 1 topics — teaching aptitude and research methods
Solve previous year UGC NET Physical Education papers
exams.what_it_leads_to: JRF (Junior Research Fellowship) for Ph.D or eligibility for Assistant Professor in Physical Education at universities and colleges across India.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Universities and colleges in Uttarakhand (HNB Garhwal, Kumaon University, LSMU) need qualified Physical Education faculty. NET qualification is mandatory for recruitment.

exams.exam_name: UPSC CSE (for IRAS/IRSE and other services)
exams.eligibility: Graduate in any discipline from a recognized university. Age: 21-32 years (relaxation for reserved categories). Attempts: 6 for General, 9 for OBC, unlimited for SC/ST (within age limit).
exams.exam_pattern: Prelims: 2 papers (GS 200 marks + CSAT 200 marks qualifying), 2 hours each. Mains: 9 papers including 4 GS, Essay, Optional Subject (2 papers), 2 language papers. Total 1750 marks. Personality Test (Interview): 275 marks.
exams.important_dates: Notification: February. Prelims: May-June. Mains: September. Interview: January-April. Final Result: April-May.
preparation_tips: Start with NCERT books for foundation in all subjects
Choose optional subject wisely based on interest and scoring potential
Practice answer writing for Mains from day one
Follow current affairs through newspapers and monthly magazines
exams.what_it_leads_to: All India Services (IAS, IPS, IFoS) and Central Services including IRAS (Indian Railway Accounts Service), IRSE (Indian Railway Service of Engineers), IFS, IRS, and 20+ other Group A services.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: IAS/IPS officers are posted in Uttarakhand districts. The state has produced notable civil servants. Mussoorie (LBSNAA) is the training academy for IAS. Uttarakhand cadre IAS postings are available.

also_known_as: National Forensic Sciences University Entrance Exam
exams.eligibility: Varies by program. For B.Sc Forensic Science: Class 12 with Science (PCM/PCB) with 50%. For M.Sc: B.Sc in relevant discipline with 55%. For other programs: Graduation in relevant field.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: MCQs on General Knowledge, Analytical Reasoning, Subject-specific questions (Science/Law/Cyber Security depending on program). Duration: 90 minutes to 2 hours. Negative marking applicable.
exams.important_dates: Notification: April-May. Exam: June-July. Counselling: July-August. Admission: August.
preparation_tips: Study basic sciences — chemistry, biology, physics from Class 11-12
Learn fundamentals of forensic science and criminology
Practice analytical reasoning and logical thinking
Stay updated on cyber security and digital forensics trends
exams.what_it_leads_to: B.Sc/M.Sc in Forensic Science, Digital Forensics, Cyber Security, Criminology, DNA Forensics, and related programs at NFSU. Careers in forensic labs, law enforcement, cybercrime units.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: NFSU has expanded to multiple campuses. Forensic science professionals are needed in Uttarakhand's police and judicial system. Growing field with national relevance.

exams.eligibility: B.E./B.Tech or equivalent in relevant engineering discipline. Age: 21-30 years (relaxation for reserved categories).
exams.exam_pattern: Prelims: Paper 1 — General Studies & Engineering Aptitude (200 marks, 2 hrs). Paper 2 — Engineering Discipline (300 marks, 3 hrs). Mains: 2 Conventional papers (600 marks total). Personality Test: 200 marks.
exams.important_dates: Notification: September. Prelims: February. Mains: June-July. Interview: October-November. Result: December.
preparation_tips: Master your core engineering subjects from B.Tech curriculum
Practice solving conventional (descriptive) engineering problems
Study General Studies — current affairs, ethics, environment
Solve previous 15-20 years ESE papers thoroughly
exams.what_it_leads_to: Group A gazetted officer posts in Indian Engineering Services including Indian Railway Service of Engineers, Central Engineering Service, Central Electrical & Mechanical Engineering Service, and more.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: ESE officers get posted in central government projects across India including infrastructure projects in Uttarakhand. BRO, CPWD projects in the state offer postings.

exams.conducting_body: NTA (National Testing Agency) on behalf of CSIR
exams.eligibility: M.Sc or equivalent in Life Sciences, Chemical Sciences, Physical Sciences, Mathematical Sciences, or Earth/Atmospheric/Ocean/Planetary Sciences with 55% (50% for reserved). BS-MS/Integrated Ph.D students also eligible.
exams.exam_pattern: CBT: Single paper, 3 hours, 200 marks. Three parts — Part A: General Aptitude (20 Qs), Part B: Subject-related MCQs (25-40 Qs), Part C: Higher value subject questions (20-25 Qs). Negative marking for Parts A and B.
exams.important_dates: June session: Registration March-April, Exam June. December session: Registration September-October, Exam December.
preparation_tips: Focus on Part C — higher marks per question, no negative marking
Study MSc-level concepts in depth for your subject
Practice Part A general aptitude for quick scoring
Revise from standard reference textbooks for your discipline
exams.what_it_leads_to: JRF (Junior Research Fellowship) for Ph.D at CSIR labs and universities, or Lectureship (Assistant Professor eligibility) at Indian universities and colleges.
exams.uttarakhand_relevance: Universities in Uttarakhand (HNB Garhwal, Kumaon, UPES, DIT) recruit faculty with NET qualification. Research opportunities at WII, FRI, and other Dehradun-based institutes.
key_subjects: Life Sciences / Chemical Sciences / Physical Sciences / Mathematical Sciences / Earth Sciences (as per chosen subject)
